element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Speed and combat design; player learns evasion and weapon management in relatively controlled opening environment
Unlimited ammo baseline weapon for learning targeting and positioning
Limited ammo resource management introduced; higher damage for strategic targeting
Most powerful, most limited ammo; player learns weapon selection strategy
Dual objective balancing: combat while protecting non-combatants; airlifting to landing zones for armor replenishment
Finite resource pressure; collection of fuel barrels creates risk-reward exploration
Health system tied to evasion skill; power-ups provide moment-to-moment relief from pressure
Larger, heavier craft designed for large-scale rescues; contrasts with Mohican speed focus
Primary combat target class; requires strategic armament selection
Departure from helicopter mechanics; rifle and missile launcher combat; internal environment navigation; major gameplay shift introducing new skill set
Third vehicle type with distinct handling; introduces land-based vehicle combat
World-scale threat; weapon fires on major cities; player transitions from tactical missions to consequence-management and facility infiltration
Final mission variant; capturing Malone alive while managing his weapon detonation threat

typical_relief_triggers: Successful hostage/prisoner airlifting to landing zone
Transition between mission phases with objective completion
typical_tension_triggers: Armor depletion from enemy fire requiring evasion
Fuel running low creating navigation pressure
Limited ammunition forcing weapon conservation decisions
Hostages under fire requiring simultaneous protection and combat
New vehicle introduction (Blackhawke, GAV) forcing relearning
Superweapon activation and city destruction sequences
On-foot missions removing helicopter safety and introducing ground-based threats
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based mission structure with escalating environmental stakes. Early missions (Hawaii/Mexico) establish core mechanics with manageable combat complexity. Mid-game (San Francisco) introduces gameplay variety via on-foot sections. Late-game (New York/Las Vegas) sustains tension through narrative consequence and final infiltration objective. Relief cycles tied to objective completion and resource replenishment rather than enemy pacing.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Fuel barrel and ammo crate placement rewards exploration beyond minimum mission path; hidden optimization routes for armor replenishment

difficulty_curve.early_game: Hawaii mission introduces all core mechanics (Mohican, three weapon types, fuel/ammo/armor management, rescue objectives) in single opening level. Manageable enemy density allows learning without overwhelming player.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mexico continues established patterns while Blackhawke variant adds rescue-focused alternate aircraft. San Francisco introduces on-foot missions and GAV, requiring complete mechanical relearning mid-campaign.
difficulty_curve.late_game: New York escalates with superweapon narrative threat and mass destruction sequences, shifting from isolated mission objectives to world-consequence management. Las Vegas finale combines all mechanics (helicopter infiltration, on-foot assault, vehicle combat) with specific capture objective against final boss.
notable_spikes: San Francisco on-foot mission introduction (mechanical shift from helicopter-only gameplay)
New York superweapon activation (narrative intensity and environmental scale jump)
Las Vegas facility infiltration with Malone confrontation (final boss encounter combining multiple vehicle types and threat management)

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard mission structure balancing offensive objectives (destroy enemy weapons/installations) with defensive objectives (rescue hostages/prisoners). Player must manage aggression and protection simultaneously.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Hawaii Mission 1 - giant mirrors theft interception + plastic surgeon rescue

notable_level_patterns.description: Each major location (Hawaii → Mexico → San Francisco → New York → Las Vegas) advances plot while introducing environmental and mechanical variety. Plastic surgeon revelation in Hawaii establishes antagonist connection; superweapon discovery escalates stakes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Hawaii through Las Vegas campaign arc

notable_level_patterns.description: Mission structure introduces three aircraft/vehicles with distinct design purposes: Mohican (speed/combat), Blackhawke (rescue capacity), GAV (ground assault). Forces player adaptation rather than single mastery.
San Francisco mission 3 removes helicopter entirely, forcing ground-based combat with rifle and missile launcher in internal environments. Creates difficulty spike and breaks established flow.

New York and Las Vegas missions shift from tactical objectives to strategic response: superweapon fires requiring damage control, mass civilian rescue, then facility infiltration. World-scale threat escalation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: New York (mass destruction response) → Las Vegas (superweapon facility infiltration)

sources_used: Wikipedia - Urban Strike (gameplay section, plot summary, development credits)
The Cutting Room Floor - Urban Strike (SNES) (platform/release information)
